title: HPKE OpModeS::Base correctly provides sender deniability
description: hpke.rs uses OpModeS::Base (no sender authentication) which means the seal does not bind the ciphertext to a specific sender key. This is intentional: sealed_broadcast hides sender identity from the relay. A contributor might suggest switching to OpModeS::Auth for sender authentication, but that would defeat the sealed-sender property. The security model comment in mls.rs room_hpke_keypair documents the accepted limitation.
